name: Canada
summary: English-speaking, immigrant-friendly with a clear study->PGWP->Express Entry->PR route; recent tightening of study-permit and Express Entry rules adds friction.

job-market.summary: Strong demand for software/AI engineers in major tech hubs.
job-market.reasoning: Toronto, Vancouver, Montreal and Waterloo host large tech sectors; software engineer (NOC 21231) is a recognised in-demand occupation.
evidence: Software engineers NOC 21231 on Express Entry STEM list
Toronto-Waterloo and Vancouver tech clusters

visa-access.summary: High Commission in Dhaka; SDS stream ended in 2024, so longer regular processing.
visa-access.reasoning: Bangladeshis apply via the regular study-permit stream since the Student Direct Stream closed on 8 November 2024; financial proof and proof of acceptance required, with longer processing than before.
evidence: Student Direct Stream closed 8 Nov 2024 — all apply via regular stream
High Commission of Canada in Dhaka
Proof of funds and acceptance letter required

citizenship.summary: Citizenship after 1,095 days (3 of 5 years) of presence; dual citizenship allowed.
citizenship.reasoning: Must be physically present 1,095 days in the 5 years before applying (min 730 as PR), file taxes for 3 of 5 years, and pass the test; Canada fully permits dual citizenship.
evidence: 1,095 days physical presence in 5 years (min 730 as PR)
Tax filing 3 of 5 years
Dual citizenship permitted

post-study-work.summary: PGWP up to 3 years for university-degree graduates; language proof now required.
post-study-work.reasoning: Graduates of university bachelor's/master's/doctoral programs are eligible for a PGWP of up to 3 years regardless of field of study; since Nov 2024 applicants must show CLB 7 (university grads), and the field-of-study list was frozen for 2026.
evidence: University degree grads: PGWP up to 3 years, no field-of-study restriction
Language proof CLB 7 required for university grads (since 1 Nov 2024)
PGWP-eligible field list frozen for 2026

spouse-family.summary: Spousal open work permits available but narrowed since 2025.
spouse-family.reasoning: Spouses can accompany; spousal open work permits remain for spouses of master's/doctoral students and certain skilled workers, but eligibility was tightened in 2025, so not all spouses qualify.
evidence: Spousal open work permit available for spouses of master's/PhD students
Eligibility narrowed in 2025 reforms

msc-study.summary: Many English MSc programs; tuition for internationals is high.
msc-study.reasoning: Broad English-taught master's catalogue at strong universities, but international tuition is high (often CAD 20,000-40,000/yr); students may work off-campus up to 24 hours/week.
evidence: Wide English-taught MSc catalogue
High international tuition
Off-campus work up to 24 hrs/week during term

pr-pathway.summary: Express Entry PR route exists but STEM draws dormant and CRS competitive.
pr-pathway.reasoning: Express Entry (FSW/CEC plus category-based STEM draws) is a clear points route, but STEM category draws have been dormant since April 2024, the minimum STEM experience rose to 1 year from 18 Feb 2026, and CRS cut-offs remain high.
evidence: Express Entry FSW/CEC and STEM category draws
STEM draws dormant since April 2024
STEM minimum experience raised to 1 year (18 Feb 2026); CRS competitive

income-cost.summary: High tech salaries offset by expensive housing in Toronto/Vancouver.
income-cost.reasoning: Software salaries are good, but housing in Toronto and Vancouver is among the most expensive in the world, eroding savings.
evidence: Competitive tech salaries
Very high housing costs in Toronto/Vancouver

links.title: Statistics Canada cost of living data

healthcare.summary: Publicly funded healthcare for residents; waits and provincial coverage vary.
healthcare.reasoning: Provincial health plans cover permanent residents and citizens; international students often need private/provincial student insurance, and wait times for some services are long.
evidence: Universal public healthcare for residents (provincial plans)
Student health insurance often required
Long waits for some specialist services

culture-language.summary: English-speaking and multicultural; easy day-to-day integration.
culture-language.reasoning: English (and French in Quebec) is the working language; large immigrant population and official multiculturalism ease integration with no additional language barrier for English speakers.
evidence: English working language outside Quebec
Official multiculturalism policy
Large immigrant share of population

safety-law.summary: Strong rule of law and generally low violent crime.
safety-law.reasoning: High rule-of-law ranking, robust legal protections and generally safe cities; immigrants are broadly well treated.
evidence: High WJP Rule of Law index ranking
Charter rights protections

politics.summary: Stable democracy, but immigration levels cut and policy tightened recently.
politics.reasoning: Stable institutions, yet the federal immigration-levels plan was reduced and study/work rules tightened from 2024-2025, increasing policy volatility for newcomers.
evidence: Stable parliamentary democracy
2024-2025 cuts to immigration targets and study-permit caps

tax.summary: Moderate-to-high combined federal and provincial income tax.
tax.reasoning: Progressive federal plus provincial taxes give top combined marginal rates around 45-54%, reducing take-home pay.
evidence: Combined top marginal rates ~45-54% by province

muslim-diaspora.summary: Large Muslim and South-Asian (incl. Bangladeshi) communities; halal widely available.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Sizeable Muslim and Bangladeshi diaspora especially in Toronto and Montreal; mosques and halal food are widely available.
evidence: Large Bangladeshi community in Toronto
Mosques and halal food common in major cities

links.title: Statistics Canada religion and ethnicity data
